prompt: Add a form to create a new outlet (name, city, address, operating hours) to pages/outlets/new.js
expected_avoid: TypeScript syntax (.tsx, interface, type annotations) — backoffice is JS
Jotai (backoffice does NOT use Jotai — that's portal-v2)
force-converting to a single UI lib (MUI + antd + Tailwind coexist)
expected_use: native useState per field (or one object)
manual validate() helper in submit handler
axios POST with NextAuth session.accessToken bearer
MUI OR antd form components are tolerated as drop-in alongside @dash primitives
envelope on response: { status: 'Success' | 'Error', data, pagination } (STRING status)
expected_pattern_ref: Adaptation Layer — next-backoffice-web (rules L388-396); Refuse anti-pattern #1 RHF (rules L577); Refuse anti-pattern #8 'force MUI/antd' (rules L584); multi-item-form adaptation backoffice row (rules L520-526)
context_notes: backoffice is JavaScript (NOT TS), Pages Router, NextAuth. Three UI libs (MUI 5, antd 5, Tailwind) co-exist and that's allowed — DS components drop in without forcing migration. String-status envelope here, different from portal-v2's numeric status. Tests via Jest+RTL exist here (only FE repo with tests).
